name: ShipStation API
what_it_does: Multi-channel order management and shipping platform API. Centralizes orders from Amazon, eBay, Shopify, and 100+ stores, then manages fulfillment, label creation, and tracking across all channels.
use_cases: Aggregating orders from multiple sales channels into a single fulfillment workflow
Automating shipping label creation with rate shopping across carriers
Managing warehouse inventory and order allocation for multi-location fulfillment
Automating shipment notifications and tracking updates to customers
Building custom order management and fulfillment agent workflows
not_for: Purely single-channel sellers with simple shipping needs
Pure carrier API access (EasyPost or Shippo are simpler for that)
Enterprise WMS with complex warehouse operations
best_when: An agent manages order fulfillment across multiple e-commerce channels (Amazon, Shopify, eBay) and needs unified shipping management and automation.
avoid_when: You only have one sales channel with simple shipping needs.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth with API key as username and API secret as password. Credentials found in ShipStation account settings under API Keys. Simple and consistent for agent use.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $9.99/month (50 shipments), Bronze $29.99 (500), Silver $59.99 (1500), Gold $99.99 (3000), Platinum $149.99 (7500), Enterprise $229.99 (unlimited)
pricing.notes: Plan limits are monthly shipment counts. All plans include API access. Clear, tiered pricing is a strength. No carrier cost markup — you pay carrier rates directly.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include ExceptionMessage field and HTTP status codes. Some errors are clear, but validation errors can be terse. Documentation doesn't always match API behavior exactly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No formal idempotency support. Order IDs are used for deduplication — same order imported twice updates the existing order.
known_agent_gotchas: 40 requests/minute rate limit is tight for high-volume order agents
Order import from stores is asynchronous — agents cannot immediately query freshly synced orders
Carrier accounts must be connected via ShipStation dashboard before API can use them
Order status transitions follow a specific workflow — agents must understand the order lifecycle
Label purchase and shipment creation are interleaved — must follow the exact multi-step process
Tags and custom fields help agents filter orders but must be configured in advance
